module.title: ABC Electronics India - SAP TM End-to-End Implementation

topic.title: Lab 8: Transportation Performance KPIs, Analytics & Management Dashboard

learningObjectives.title: Interpret transportation KPIs
learningObjectives.description: Assess delivery, cost, utilization and incident metrics for business performance.

learningObjectives.title: Perform root cause analysis
learningObjectives.description: Link operational incidents to underlying process gaps and system controls.

learningObjectives.title: Present management-level conclusions
learningObjectives.description: Balance outcome success with operational stability and risk exposure.

learningObjectives.title: Define improvement roadmap
learningObjectives.description: Recommend preventive controls and monitoring actions for continuous optimization.

sections.title: Real-Time Lab 8: Transportation Performance KPIs & Management Dashboard

sections.content: The transportation cycle has been completed successfully. Senior management has requested a performance review to understand why transportation costs increased, whether customer commitments were met and what improvements can be implemented for future shipments. As the SAP TM Consultant, you must analyze operational KPIs and present recommendations.

sections.title: Your Role
sections.content: You are preparing a weekly Transportation Performance Review for the Logistics Director using SAP TM analytics and operational KPIs.

The shipment experienced multiple events during its lifecycle including master data issues, carrier unavailability, warehouse loading delays, vehicle breakdown and customer-requested delivery changes. Management wants to know whether these incidents affected overall logistics performance.

items: Why were there five production incidents?
Which incident caused the highest business risk?
Could transportation costs have been reduced?
Which department caused the most delays?
What preventive actions should be implemented?

sections.content: The Logistics Director asks: 'Although five production incidents occurred, the customer still received the shipment on time and transportation cost remained within the approved budget. Should we consider this shipment successful or unsuccessful?'

Recommend implementing automated master data validation before planning, introducing secondary carrier agreements, integrating warehouse quality status with SAP TM, monitoring vehicle health proactively and using automated freight invoice validation. These improvements will reduce operational incidents while maintaining current customer service levels.

sections.title: Consultant Best Practice
sections.content: KPIs should not be viewed individually. A consultant evaluates the complete business outcome by balancing customer satisfaction, transportation cost, operational efficiency, risk and resource utilization.

sections.title: Real Production Example
sections.content: A global FMCG company reduced transportation incidents by 42% after implementing KPI dashboards that tracked carrier performance, warehouse delays, planning exceptions and invoice disputes. Management reviewed these dashboards weekly to drive continuous process improvements.

sections.title: Accenture Scenario Question
sections.content: Management asks whether a transportation process with multiple operational issues but successful customer delivery should be considered a success. A consultant should explain that business success depends on overall outcomes while also highlighting process improvement opportunities identified through KPI analysis.

items: Project continues to Lab 9: Hypercare - Priority 1 Production Issue.
